values: Being slow can cause you to struggle to keep pace with the team
Not being fit can cause you to miss out on the deep, transformative experience Indiahikes designs

evidence.quote_or_summary: You struggle to be a part of the team because you're slow.
You miss out on a deep, transformative experience that Indiahikes specially designs for you.

values: Walk, jog, or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Trekking primarily uses lungs, core, calves, glutes, quads, and hamstrings

evidence.quote_or_summary: To do the Everest Base Camp Trek (classic route) comfortably, you must be able to walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes. This is the minimum recommended fitness level that you must aim for.
Trekking is a sport that primarily uses your lungs, core, calves, glutes, quads and hamstrings.

values: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
Only running, jogging, or walking are accepted as fitness proofs; cross-training workouts are not considered valid
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the trek's fitness criteria
Upload a public profile link from a fitness tracking app (e.g., Strava, Nike Run Club) on your Indiahikes account
Fitness records may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month

evidence.quote_or_summary: Every trekker needs fitness approval from the Indiahikes team 20 days before the trek date. Without this, you will not be allowed on the trek.
We accept only running, jogging or walking as fitness proofs. Cross-training workouts will not be considered valid.
So make sure to record at least 3 runs from the last 1 month to meet the fitness criteria of the trek.
Paste your profile's link on your Indiahikes account (your profile has to be public).
If you fail to link your fitness profile; If you do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month

values: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
A 6-week plan is provided for those not currently active

evidence.quote_or_summary: All you need to do is start following the fitness routine 6 to 8 weeks before your trek begins.
If you are not currently active, follow the below schedule to get trek fit in 6 weeks!

values: Children below 14 years are not required to submit fitness records
Parents must ensure that children below 14 remain physically active and prepared for the trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: Children below 14 years are not required to submit fitness records.
However, parents must ensure that children remain physically active and prepared for the trek.
